title: On the Criteria To Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules
main_claim: Decompose by information hiding (each module hides a design decision), not by processing steps
limitations: One worked example (KWIC); no quantitative metric
applicability: Basis for hooks/lib split: hide hook-protocol decision behind one module

title: Structured Design
authors: W.P. Stevens; G.J. Myers; L.L. Constantine
main_claim: Origin of coupling/cohesion as ordinal design heuristics
applicability: Terminology baseline; warns against treating levels as measurements

title: OO Design Quality Metrics: An Analysis of Dependencies
venue: OOPSLA'94 workshop position paper; formalized in Agile Software Development (2002)
main_claim: Defines Ca, Ce, I=Ce/(Ca+Ce), A, D; Stable Dependencies / Stable Abstractions Principles
limitations: Definitional/normative; no empirical validation; not peer reviewed
applicability: I computed for modules; used directionally (depend toward stability), not with thresholds

title: A Metrics Suite for Object Oriented Design
main_claim: CK suite incl. LCOM (method pairs not sharing fields)
limitations: LCOM representation condition contested; weak discrimination
applicability: LCOM inapplicable as-is to function-module Python hooks; used only as concept

title: Measuring Coupling and Cohesion in Object-Oriented Systems
main_claim: Cohesion as connected components of method/attribute graph (basis of LCOM4)
limitations: Symposium paper; 'LCOM4' name applied later by tool vendors
applicability: Connected-components idea reused for module-level cohesion proxy

title: A Validation of Object-Oriented Design Metrics as Quality Indicators
authors: V.R. Basili; L.C. Briand; W.L. Melo
main_claim: CBO/RFC/DIT predict fault-proneness; LCOM insignificant
applicability: Evidence that coupling metrics carry signal, cohesion metrics weak

title: Empirical evidence on the link between OO measures and external quality attributes: SLR
authors: R. Jabangwe; J. Borstler; D. Smite; C. Wohlin
main_claim: SLR of 99 studies: LOC/WMC/RFC/CBO strongest links; LCOM weak/inconsistent
applicability: Grounds refusal to use universal LCOM thresholds

title: Finding and evaluating community structure in networks
main_claim: Modularity Q as partition quality function
applicability: Q computed for all partitions incl. directory baseline

title: Resolution limit in community detection
main_claim: Modularity optimization cannot detect communities below ~sqrt(E) scale
applicability: Why small clusters (C6-C8, n=2) must not be over-interpreted

title: Fast unfolding of communities in large networks
authors: V.D. Blondel; J.-L. Guillaume; R. Lambiotte; E. Lefebvre
main_claim: Louvain: scalable greedy modularity optimization
applicability: Primary clustering algorithm (5 seeds)

title: From Louvain to Leiden: guaranteeing well-connected communities
authors: V.A. Traag; L. Waltman; N.J. van Eck
main_claim: Louvain can yield badly/disconnected communities; Leiden guarantees connected
applicability: Motivates stability check across seeds; spot-checked directly (WebFetch)

title: Normalized Cuts and Image Segmentation
main_claim: Normalized cut avoids min-cut small-segment bias; spectral relaxation
limitations: NP-complete exact; transfer to software graphs is by analogy
applicability: Grounds H1 verdict: raw min-cut biased, need normalized criteria

title: Exploring the Structure of Complex Software Designs
authors: A. MacCormack; J. Rusnak; C.Y. Baldwin
main_claim: DSM + propagation cost compare architectures; redesign measurably reduced coupling
applicability: Precedent for DSM artifact and propagation-style metrics

title: Detection of Logical Coupling Based on Product Release History
authors: H. Gall; K. Hajek; M. Jazayeri
main_claim: Origin of logical (change) coupling from co-change history
applicability: Method basis for layer 9.2 change-coupling graph

title: Decoupling Level: A New Metric for Architectural Maintenance Complexity
authors: R. Mo; Y. Cai; R. Kazman; L. Xiao; Q. Feng
main_claim: DL metric validated against maintenance effort on 100+ projects
applicability: Supports multi-metric (not single-scalar) assessment

title: On the Relationship Between Change Coupling and Software Defects
authors: M. D'Ambros; M. Lanza; R. Robbes
main_claim: Change coupling correlates with defects beyond structural metrics
applicability: Justifies weighting co-change evidence in hotspot ranking

title: Import Linter documentation
main_claim: Python import contracts: forbidden, protected, layers, independence, acyclic siblings, custom
applicability: Primary fitness-function tool for this repo; spot-checked directly

title: Grimp documentation
main_claim: Queryable Python import graphs; engine under import-linter
applicability: Alternative graph builder to validate custom AST scan

title: dependency-cruiser
main_claim: JS/TS dependency validation with forbidden rules, cycles, orphans
applicability: Reference for rule taxonomy only

title: ArchUnit User Guide v1.4.2
main_claim: Architecture tests: layers, cycles, dependency/naming rules
applicability: Pattern donor for test-as-fitness-function style

title: pydeps documentation
main_claim: Python dependency visualization + cycle detection
applicability: Optional visualization of dependency-graph.graphml alternative

title: Building Evolutionary Architectures, 2nd ed.
authors: N. Ford; R. Parsons; P. Kua; P. Sadalage
main_claim: Architectural fitness functions: automated guiding measures (atomic/holistic, triggered/continual)
limitations: Direct fetch paywalled; verified via TOC/edition pages
applicability: Framework for section 11 fitness functions

title: Domain-Driven Design
main_claim: Bounded Context: explicit boundary of model consistency
limitations: Pre-microservices; service mapping is later interpretation
applicability: Basis of section 08 boundary analysis

title: Monolith to Microservices
main_claim: Coupling taxonomy: implementation, temporal, deployment, domain
applicability: Vocabulary for coupling typing in sections 05/12

title: Software Architecture Metrics
authors: Ciceri; Farley; Ford; Harmel-Law; Keeling; Lilienthal; et al.
main_claim: Ch.4 Modularity Maturity Index (Lilienthal): debt triage refactor/replace/leave
limitations: Anthology; MMI from author's own method
applicability: Supports multi-metric maturity framing

title: Balancing Coupling in Software Design
main_claim: Integration Strength (intrusive/functional/model/contract) x distance x volatility
limitations: New; no independent empirical validation yet
applicability: Model used to grade file-store contracts (model coupling)

title: Deconstructing the Monolith (+ Under Deconstruction, 2020)
main_claim: Modular monolith with enforced component boundaries (Wedge; later packwerk)
limitations: Outcomes mostly qualitative; 2020 post reports enforcement difficulties
applicability: Direct template for Option B (modular monolith + boundary tooling); spot-checked

title: Goodbye Microservices
main_claim: 140+ services consolidated back into monolith; productivity recovered
limitations: Fan-out workload specifics; trade-off tale per authors
applicability: Counter-evidence to H7 radical isolation; supports Option C caution

title: Introducing Domain-Oriented Microservice Architecture (DOMA)
main_claim: 2200 microservices grouped into domains/layers with gateways to cut complexity
limitations: Sparse quantified outcomes; author attribution via secondary sources
applicability: Layered-domain grouping pattern for hooks domains

title: Scaling up the Prime Video audio/video monitoring service and reducing costs by 90%
main_claim: Distributed serverless tool refactored to single process: -90% cost
limitations: Original URL dead; interpretation contested (serverless-misfit, not anti-microservices)
applicability: Evidence that boundary count has real runtime/cost price (H7)

title: Why Google Stores Billions of Lines of Code in a Single Repository
main_claim: Monorepo enables atomic cross-cutting changes and one-version dependency management
limitations: Requires massive custom infra; visibility can increase unwanted coupling
applicability: Monorepo != modularity: explicit dependency rules still required
